Q6 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following is not an application of a gray code?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Gray Code is a binary code where two successive values differ in only one bit. It is used in
transmission of digital signal as it reduces occurrence of error.
• The cyclic property of gray code makes it useful for angle measurement.
• It is generally used as a position indicator as two successive values differ in only one bit.
• Gray code does not use positional weighting, making it unsuitable for arithmetic operations.

Q8 Not checked

A n-bit ADC has following specifications: 16-bit counter, clock rate = 4 MHz, input voltage = 12 V and capacitor = 0.47 μF.
If the maximum integrator output voltage is –7 V when the counter have cycled through 2n counts, then the value of resistor in the integrator is _______ × 103 Ω. (upto two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 59.74
Solution

59.74 (57.00 - 61.00)

Solution
